expectations.text: Output contains all 4 rounds (Round 1 through Round 4)
expectations.evidence: All 4 rounds present: Round 1 Visionary Proposes, Round 2 Skeptic Challenges, Round 3 Visionary Defends, Round 4 Pragmatist Decides

expectations.text: Visionary, Skeptic, and Pragmatist personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: Visionary proposes acquisition, Skeptic raises 7 challenges, Pragmatist decides with modify-and-proceed

expectations.text: Round 2 challenges are specific to the proposal, referencing details like cost, integration risk, or team dynamics
expectations.evidence: Challenges reference $3M = 37.5% ARR, integration risk, retention risk, scoping the problem, opportunity cost, cultural contamination, aggressive timeline

expectations.text: Round 3 references specific challenge numbers from Round 2
expectations.evidence: Round 3 responds to each numbered challenge with Concede/Counter labels

expectations.text: Round 4 contains one of the 4 valid verdicts (proceed/modify-and-proceed/defer/reject)
Debate metrics table with all 5 metrics filled in
expectations.evidence: Table includes challengesRaised: 7, challengesSurvived: 3, challengesDefeated: 0, challengesConceded: 4, confidenceScore: 7

expectations.text: Numbered action items with concrete next steps
expectations.evidence: 7 numbered action items with owners, specific tasks, and deadlines

expectations.text: Round 4 rationale references arguments from both Visionary and Skeptic
expectations.evidence: Pragmatist references Visionary's core argument about time-to-capability and Skeptic's challenges on price, timeline, and retention
